questions.question: When should someone seek professional help instead of relying solely on collective activity?

options.text: Never — collective activity can replace all treatment
When experiencing severe symptoms, clinical conditions, or safety concerns requiring professional assessment
Only after trying for many years

questions.explanation: This course provides psychoeducation, not clinical treatment. Professional help is needed for severe depression, suicidality, clinical-level symptoms, safety concerns, or when self-management strategies aren't sufficient. Collective activity can complement professional care but shouldn't replace it when professional care is indicated.

title: Protecting Mental Health in Sport — Toxic Environments - Quiz
